2603.18573.title: Interplay: Training Independent Simulators for Reference-Free Conversational Recommendation
2603.18573.paper_summary: Training conversational recommender systems (CRS) requires extensive dialogue data, which is challenging to collect at scale. To address this, researchers have used simulated user-recommender conversations. Traditional simulation approaches often utilize a single large language model (LLM) that generates entire conversations with prior knowledge of the target items, leading to scripted and artificial dialogues. We propose a reference-free simulation framework that trains two independent LLMs, one as the user and one as the conversational recommender. These models interact in real-time without access to predetermined target items, but preference summaries and target attributes, enabling the recommender to genuinely infer user preferences through dialogue. This approach produces more realistic and diverse conversations that closely mirror authentic human-AI interactions. Our reference-free simulators match or exceed existing methods in quality, while offering a scalable solution for generating high-quality conversational recommendation data without constraining conversations to pre-defined target items. We conduct both quantitative and human evaluations to confirm the effectiveness of our reference-free approach.
2603.18573.paper_summary_zh: 訓練對話推薦系統 (CRS) 需要大量的對話數據，這在大規模收集上是具有挑戰性的。為了解決這個問題，研究人員使用了模擬的用戶-推薦者對話。傳統的模擬方法通常利用一個大型語言模型 (LLM)，該模型在了解目標項目的前提下生成整個對話，導致對話顯得生硬和不自然。我們提出了一個無參考的模擬框架，訓練兩個獨立的 LLM，一個作為用戶，另一個作為對話推薦者。這些模型在實時互動中，無需訪問預先確定的目標項目，而是使用偏好摘要和目標屬性，使推薦者能夠通過對話真實地推斷用戶的偏好。這種方法產生了更真實和多樣化的對話，與真實的人類-AI 互動非常相似。我們的無參考模擬器在質量上與現有方法相匹配或超越，同時提供了一個可擴展的解決方案，用於生成高質量的對話推薦數據，而不將對話限制於預定的目標項目。我們進行了定量和人類評估，以確認我們的無參考方法的有效性。
2603.18573.author: Jerome Ramos et.al.
2603.18573.authors: Jerome Ramos, Feng Xia, Xi Wang, Shubham Chatterjee, Xiao Fu, Hossein A. Rahmani, Aldo Lipani
